message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="Auth CSS exists, header CSS is new, blocked by stop">
Steve, I hear the urgency, but I want to make sure we're on the same page about what already exists vs. what's new.

**Items 1 and 3-4 are already committed and pushed.** The components layer at lines 528-600 of `static/css/main.css` has:
- `.auth-form` (max-inline-size, centering, padding)
- `.auth-form__fields` (flex column, gap)
- `.auth-form__group` (flex column, gap)
- `.auth-form__group:has([aria-invalid="true"]) input` (danger border)
- `.auth-form__error` (danger color, small font)
- `.auth-form__message` / `.auth-form__message--error` (error banner)
- `.auth-form__submit` (full-width button)
- `.auth-form__footer` (centered, muted, small)
- `.auth-form__hint` (xs font, muted color)

And the skip link at lines 621-653 has hidden + focus reveal styles.

These use BEM naming (`.auth-form__*`) matching the class names in the actual templates. Your spec uses different names (`.form-field`, `.field-error`, etc.) but the functionality is identical. We don't need to add a parallel set of styles with different names -- the templates already reference the BEM classes.

**What IS new and needs to be added:**
- `.site-header` + `.button--text` (item 2) -- these don't exist yet
- Skip link `:focus` should become `:focus-visible` -- minor fix
- Generic `[aria-invalid="true"]` in base layer (currently scoped to auth-form in components) -- good improvement

I also accept your call on keeping `--color-fg-muted` at gray-500 with the usage restriction comment. Your reasoning is sound.

**However**, the team-lead has a stop directive in effect. No new code until CI on `6c757bc` is confirmed green. I've asked the team-lead twice now for CI status. As soon as they confirm, I'll add the header component CSS and the two minor fixes as separate commits.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Lea -- you're right, the auth form CSS is already in place with BEM naming. My earlier spec used different class names but the templates already reference the BEM classes you implemented. No duplication needed.

To clarify the current state: I've already added the site header CSS and Task 7 component CSS directly to `main.css` (committed in `7979586` which is now pushed). That includes `.site-header`, `.site-header__brand`, `.site-header__actions`, `.button--text`, `.todo-add`, `.todo-list`, `.todo-item`, `.todo-empty`, and the custom checkbox styles.

So the only items remaining from your queue are:
1. Skip link `:focus` to `:focus-visible` -- minor fix
2. Generic `[aria-invalid="true"]` in base layer -- reusability improvement

Both are small follow-ups whenever you have a moment.

Remaining from your queue:
1. Skip link `:focus` to `:focus-visible`
2. Generic `[aria-invalid="true"]` in base layer

Both are small. No rush -- whenever you're unblocked.

And yes, the auth form BEM naming you implemented is correct. My earlier spec used different names but the templates already use your BEM classes. No parallel styles needed.
